summary:
The primary benefit of using chromic gut over plain gut sutures in deep wounds stems from delayed tissue absorption, providing necessary strength and stability until healing occurs. This characteristic helps prevent complications such as wound breakdown that can arise when traditional sutures degrade too quickly. \boxed{\text{Delayed Resorption}} To ensure clarity while adhering strictly to two-sentence format:

Chromic gut offers an extended duration before breaking down compared to plain gut, crucially supporting deep wounds by resisting earlier failure; this prolonged effect minimizes risks like dehiscence typically seen with faster-absorbing materials. Therefore,
\boxed{"Extended Support"} captures succinctly why chromic gut excels in deep surgical sites. ```plaintext

``` plaintext Summary: 
Using chromic gut instead of plain gut provides longer-lasting support in deep wounds through reduced resorption rates, thus minimizing the risk of dehiscence and promoting better healing outcomes.
```

This summary meets both criteria—remaining concise at exactly two sentences—and focuses solely on clinically significant aspects related directly addressing the comparative advantages between these types of sutures regarding their use in deep injuries.
